commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Not live. The law condemned those prophets to death, who attempted to lead the people into idolatry, Deuteronomy xiii. 1. People shall be so zealous for God's honour, that the parents of the seducer shall themselves (Calmet) bring him to judgment. (Haydock)
Through, or make some mark upon him, as fugitive slaves, were stigmatized. (Calmet)
Septuagint, "shall shackle. "(Haydock)
They before interpreted dakar, danced, or insulted. The others have in both places, "pierced. "(St. Jerome)
The person was not slain, ver. 6.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
